_comment: SA-B4 Crimson Reach Black Market voice content. Floor Manager (Vex Tarn) auctioneer-lines in his impatient, declarative register (the antithesis of Velo's ceremonial cadence). Salko's flat-bid template mirrors the SA-B3 Stellaris file verbatim; Reach reads its own file so the venue separation stays clean. Reach Buyer ambient post-session lines for the 3 ambient personas. Sable reads register the floor's sharper rhythm. Voice-checked against character_voices.md and dialogue_writing_guide.md; no ceremonial keywords; average sentence length <= 10 words across the auctioneer_lines surface (locked decision §B4.5).

rival_won: Salko picks up his catalogue. He logs the price. That is all.
player_won: Salko sets the catalogue down. He files it. He does not look up.
no_overlap: Salko sat through the session. He bid where he chose. The lots that brought him were not yours.
absent_retired: Salko was not in the room today. The seat he prefers stayed open.

reach_buyer: A buyer counts crates against a manifest. She closes the manifest and walks out without speaking.
A buyer pays in stamped credits and a sealed envelope. Vex takes both without looking at the envelope.
Two buyers exchange a glance over the last lot. Neither bid on it. Neither volunteers why.
A buyer leaves the floor with a handcart and a tarp. The tarp is the same color as the crate beneath it.

sable_reads.ceiling_correct: The room reads cleaner here. The bids land where I called them.
sable_reads.ceiling_off: I missed the read on two ceilings. The Reach moves faster than the Port. I will adjust.
sable_reads.no_rivals_attended: No named rivals on the floor. The room ran on ambient buyers tonight.

empty_state: Vex: "Floor opens when there's enough on it. Won't be long. Check back."
retired_rival: {display_name} has not been on the floor in a while.
tier_locked: The floor is for members. Talk to the Guild first.
